name: debugging-strategies
description: Master systematic debugging techniques, profiling tools, and root cause analysis to efficiently track down bugs across any codebase or technology stack. Use when investigating bugs, performance issues, or unexpected behavior.
capabilities: Apply scientific method to debugging for systematic root cause discovery
Use binary search debugging to isolate problematic code efficiently
Configure debuggers in VS Code, Chrome DevTools, and language-specific tools
Analyze memory leaks and performance bottlenecks using profilers
Debug production incidents safely with proper logging and monitoring techniques
Debug distributed systems by tracing requests across services
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Security scanner flagged 48 potential issues in markdown documentation. After evaluation, all findings are false positives. The flagged patterns are code examples and documentation within markdown files, not executable code. External commands shown are educational CLI examples (git bisect, etc.). Network references are localhost debugging endpoints. This is a legitimate debugging education skill with no security concerns.
valueStatement: Transform frustrating debugging sessions into systematic problem-solving using proven techniques, powerful tools, and methodical approaches that work across any technology stack.
limitations: Cannot debug issues without reproducible symptoms or observable behavior
Cannot access external systems or logs not provided in the conversation
Cannot perform live debugging of running processes without proper tooling setup
Cannot guarantee finding root cause for truly random or hardware-level issues

useCases.targetUser: DevOps engineers and on-call developers handling production incidents
useCases.title: Debug Production Incidents
useCases.description: Investigate and diagnose production bugs using systematic approaches, gather evidence safely without making changes, and identify root causes for rapid resolution.

useCases.title: Fix Performance Bottlenecks
useCases.description: Profile applications to identify slow operations, analyze memory usage patterns, and optimize code based on actual performance data rather than guesses.

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers dealing with hard-to-reproduce issues
useCases.title: Track Down Elusive Bugs
useCases.description: Apply binary search, differential debugging, and trace analysis to find bugs that only appear under specific conditions or in production environments.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Debug Request
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm seeing this error: [ERROR_MESSAGE]. The error occurs when [WHAT_TRIGGERS_IT]. Can you help me apply systematic debugging steps to find the root cause?
promptTemplates.scenario: When you encounter a specific error and need guidance on how to approach debugging it

promptTemplates.title: Performance Issue Investigation
promptTemplates.prompt: My [APPLICATION/COMPONENT] is running slowly. It takes [TIME] to complete [OPERATION]. I tried [WHAT_YOU_TRIED]. Help me use profiling tools to find the bottleneck.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have a performance problem and need structured debugging approach

promptTemplates.title: Intermittent Bug Hunt
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a bug that happens [FREQUENCY] but I cannot reproduce it reliably. It seems to affect [WHAT_IT_AFFECTS]. What strategies can I use to track this down?
promptTemplates.scenario: When dealing with bugs that are hard to reproduce consistently

promptTemplates.title: Production Debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me debug a production issue safely. I can see [ERROR/SYMPTOM] in [WHERE_YOU_CAN_SEE_IT]. What should I check first and how do I avoid making things worse?
promptTemplates.scenario: When investigating production issues where caution is critical

bestPractices: Always reproduce the issue locally before attempting fixes to ensure you understand the problem
Change one thing at a time during debugging to isolate what actually fixes the issue
Document your findings and hypotheses as you go to help future debugging sessions
Use version control history (git bisect) to find when regressions were introduced
antiPatterns: Making multiple changes at once without tracking what fixed the issue
Assuming the problem is complex when it could be a simple typo or configuration issue
Ignoring error messages and stack traces - they usually contain valuable clues
Debugging in production without proper monitoring or rollback plans

faq.question: What is the first step when debugging any issue?
faq.answer: Reproduce the issue consistently. You cannot fix what you cannot reproduce. Gather exact steps that trigger the problem and document environment details.

faq.question: How do I debug intermittent bugs that are hard to reproduce?
faq.answer: Add extensive logging to capture state when the bug occurs. Look for patterns in when it happens (timing, specific inputs, load conditions). Use stress testing to increase chances of reproduction.

faq.question: When should I use logging vs a debugger?
faq.answer: Use debuggers for complex runtime state inspection and stepping through code. Use logging for production issues, timing-related problems, and when you cannot attach a debugger.

faq.question: How do I find memory leaks in my application?
faq.answer: Take heap snapshots before and after operations. Compare snapshots to find growing object counts. Common causes: event listeners not removed, closures holding references, or growing caches/arrays.

faq.question: What is binary search debugging?
faq.answer: A technique where you divide your code or search space in half repeatedly to isolate the problematic section. Comment out half the code to determine which half contains the bug, then repeat.

faq.question: How do I debug production issues safely?
faq.answer: Gather evidence first without making changes. Use existing logs and monitoring. Form hypotheses and test them in staging. Add new logging or metrics before deploying fixes. Always have rollback plans.
